etymology.description: 變(變) (biàn, "change") + 化(化) (huà, "transform") = change; transformation
etymology.interpretation: The word 變化(變化) (biànhuà, "change") combines 變(變) (biàn, "change"), which implies an alteration or modification, with 化(化) (huà, "transform"), suggesting a transformation or the process of changing. Together, they describe a process or result of change or transformation.

examples.chinese: 社会变化很快(社會變化很快)，经济随之调整(經濟隨之調整)。
examples.pinyin: shèhuì biànhuà hěn kuài, jīngjì suí zhī tiáozhěng.
examples.english: Society changes rapidly, and the economy adjusts accordingly.

examples.chinese: 古往今来变化无常(古往今來變化無常)。
examples.english: Change has been unpredictable throughout the ages.
